---
title: "Journal Entry 1: Research Idea"
date: "2026-01-15"
categories: [research-idea, planning]
description: "What is your research idea?"
---

![Research planning](../thumbnail.jpg)

## What is your research idea?

My research idea is to build a pricing simulation that shows how prices change over time based on supply, demand, and price elasticity. The main problem I want to address is that many economics models are taught using static graphs, which do not clearly show how prices adjust step by step in real markets.

This problem is important because students often understand the final equilibrium point but struggle to understand the process that leads to it. A dynamic simulation can help bridge the gap between theory and real-world market behavior by making price changes easier to visualize and experiment with.

My initial approach is to design a simple model where users can input supply, demand, and elasticity values and observe how prices adjust gradually over time instead of instantly reaching equilibrium. The simulation will focus on clarity and learning rather than perfect realism.

I plan to use Python to implement the model and create a basic interactive interface for users. Graphs will be used to show price changes over time so that trends and adjustments are easy to see.

Through this project, I hope to create an educational tool that helps students better understand core pricing concepts and gain intuition about how markets respond to changes in supply and demand.

One relevant published research paper in this area is Marshall’s work on supply and demand, which provides foundational ideas about market equilibrium and price adjustment.
